heroHeadline: Pakistan Kills 71 TTP Militants in North Waziristan Infiltration; Non-Escalation Holds Day 16
heroSubtitle: Pakistan's armed forces killed at least 71 TTP militants in heavy clashes that began April 25 as a cross-border infiltration column attempted to enter North Waziristan from Afghanistan — the largest TTP incursion since the Urumqi non-escalation framework took hold April 9. The Taliban-Pakistan inter-state framework marked its 16th consecutive day without major cross-border military exchanges, sustaining the longest lull since Operation Ghazab-il-Haq began February 26. UN OCHA confirmed the first humanitarian aid delivery to Nuristan Province in over six weeks, reaching conflict-affected communities cut off since early March. Pakistan continues deporting 147,000+ Afghans in 2026 — a record single-day pace of 6,148 on one day — as Urumqi Round 2 remains expected mid-to-late May.
footerNote: Data sourced from UNAMA, SIGAR, ACLED, Brown University Costs of War Project, US DoD, Reuters, BBC, Tolo News, Dawn, Al Jazeera, HRW, OHCHR
